---
title: "Sim_setup"
format: html
---

# Packages

```{r}
# install.packages("dsims", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
library(tidyverse)
library(dsims)
library(Distance)
library(dssd)
library(dsm)
library(sf)
library(mgcv)
library(qrng) 
library(furrr)
library(future)
```

# Import Functions

```{r}
source(here::here("Functions4.R"))
```

# Constants

```{r}
true_N <- 14000

points_or_lines = "point"

trunc_dist <- 200
scale_parameter <- 70
design_spacing <- 10000/sqrt(2*10)

density_grid_spacing <- 100

# design_angle <- 0
bootstrap_reps <- 200

outer_loop_reps <- 100
```

# Code

## Region

```{r}
# region <- make_region(region_sf = NULL, 0, 5000, 0, 5000, "m")
# plot(region, main = "Survey Region")
```

# Replicating Fewster Triangle regions

```{r}
# Define a right triangle with 1000m axes
triangle_coords <- matrix(c(
  10000, 10000, 
  10000, 0, 
  0, 0, 
  10000, 10000       
), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE)

region <- st_polygon(list(triangle_coords)) |> 
  st_sfc() |> 
  st_sf(geometry = _)

region <- make_region(region_sf = region)

plot(region)
```

## Density

```{r}
# my_hotspots <- list(
#   list(centre = c(2000, 8000), sigma = 800, amplitude = 1.2),
#   list(centre = c(5000, 2000), sigma = 6000, amplitude = 0.5),
#   list(centre = c(8000, 9000), sigma = 1000, amplitude = 2)
# )

my_hotspots1 <- list(
  list(centre = c(5000, 5000), sigma = 80000, amplitude = 1)
)

density_true1 <- make_hotspot_density(region, my_hotspots1)



my_hotspots2 <- list(
  list(centre = c(0, 0), sigma = 2000, amplitude = 10)
)

density_true2 <- make_hotspot_density(region, my_hotspots2)



my_hotspots3 <- list(
  list(centre = c(10000, 0), sigma = 4000, amplitude = 1),
  list(centre = c(10000, 2500), sigma = 4000, amplitude = 1),
  list(centre = c(10000, 5000), sigma = 4000, amplitude = 1),
  list(centre = c(10000, 7500), sigma = 4000, amplitude = 1),
  list(centre = c(10000, 10000), sigma = 4000, amplitude = 1)
)

density_true3 <- make_hotspot_density(region, my_hotspots3)



my_hotspots4 <- list(
  list(centre = c(10000, 0), sigma = 10000, amplitude = 1)
)

density_true4 <- make_hotspot_density(region, my_hotspots4)

densities <- list(density_true1, density_true2, density_true3, density_true4)
```

```{r}
ggplot() +
  # Assuming the first list element contains the sf density grid
  geom_sf(data = density_true1@density.surface[[1]], aes(fill = density), color = NA) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  geom_sf(data = region@region, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 1) +
  theme_minimal()
```


# Test Survey

```{r, message=FALSE}
# Test Survey
test_region <- region
test_density_true <- density_true1

# 1. Generate the true population locations
test_sim_truth <- generate_simulated_pop(
  region = test_region,
  density_true = test_density_true,
  N = true_N,
  scale_param = scale_parameter,
  truncation = trunc_dist
)

# 2. Run a single survey on THAT exact population
test_survey <- survey_data(
  region = test_region,
  realized_population = test_sim_truth$population,
  transect_type = points_or_lines,
  spacing = design_spacing,
  truncation = trunc_dist
)

# 3. Fit the distance model to THAT survey data
test_ds <- fit_ds(
  region = test_region,
  dist_data = test_survey$dist_data,
  obsdata = test_survey$obsdata,
  segdata = test_survey$segdata,
  transect_type = points_or_lines,
  truncation = trunc_dist
)

# View the detection function
plot(test_ds$ds_model)
```

```{r}
# Extract the unique IDs of animals that were detected during this specific survey run
detected_ids <- test_survey$dist_data$individual[!is.na(test_survey$dist_data$distance)]

# Use ggplot2 to safely layer S4 spatial slots and avoid dispatch errors
ggplot() +
  # 1. Base layer: The study region
  geom_sf(data = test_region@region, fill = "white", color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  
  # 2. Animal layer: True population, highlighting detected animals in red
  geom_point(data = test_sim_truth$population@population |> 
               mutate(Detected = individual %in% detected_ids),
             aes(x = x, y = y, color = Detected), size = 1, alpha = 0.7) +
             
  # 3. Transects layer: Call the samplers directly from the list object
  geom_sf(data = test_survey$transects@samplers, color = "blue", linewidth = 0.8) +
  
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "grey80", "TRUE" = "red")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Simulated Survey Execution",
       subtitle = paste("Transect Type:", points_or_lines, "| Blue: Samplers | Red: Detected"),
       x = "X", y = "Y")
```

```{r}
plan(multisession)
```


```{r}
mean_detections <- function(N, 
                            reps = 100, 
                            density = density_true1,
                            survey_region = region,
                            scale = scale_parameter,
                            trunc = trunc_dist,
                            transect = points_or_lines,
                            spacing = design_spacing) {
  
    dets <- future_map_dbl(
    seq_len(reps),
    \(i) {
      
      sim_truth <- generate_simulated_pop(
        region = survey_region,
        density_true = density,
        N = N,
        scale_param = scale,
        truncation = trunc
      )
      
      survey <- survey_data(
        region = survey_region,
        realized_population = sim_truth$population,
        transect_type = transect,
        spacing = spacing,
        truncation = trunc
      )
      
      sum(
        !is.na(survey$dist_data$distance) &
          survey$dist_data$distance <= trunc
      )
    },
    .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE)
  )
  
  # Return named vector for easier reading in the console
  res <- c(Mean_Detections = mean(dets), SD = sd(dets))
  return(res)
}
```

```{r, message=FALSE}
mean_detections(N = true_N, 
                reps = 5, 
                density = density_true2,
                survey_region = region,
                scale = scale_parameter,
                trunc = trunc_dist,
                transect = points_or_lines,
                spacing = design_spacing)
```


```{r}
sim_grid <- expand_grid(
  target_dets = c(30, 120, 300),
  n_transects = c(10, 20, 30, 50)
) |> 
  mutate(
    spacing_val = 10000 / sqrt(2*n_transects)
  )

find_optimal_N <- function(target_n, spacing_val) {
  
  # The Objective Function: optim() will try to minimize this value
  objective_func <- function(N_guess) {
    
    # Run the fast simulation with the current guess for N
    sim_res <- mean_detections(
      N = round(N_guess), 
      spacing = spacing_val,
      reps = 20,  
      density = density_true1,
      transect = "point"
    )
    
    # Return the squared error (Difference between simulated detections and our target)
    sq_error <- (sim_res["Mean_Detections"] - target_n)^2
    return(sq_error)
  }
  
  # Use optim with the "Brent" method, which is specifically designed for 1-Dimensional bounds
  opt_res <- optim(
    par = 5000,           # Initial starting guess for N
    fn = objective_func,
    method = "Brent",
    lower = 50,           # Minimum possible population
    upper = 50000,        # Maximum possible population bounds (increase if needed)
    control = list(abstol = 5) # Stop when we are very close to the target
  )
  
  # Return the best N rounded to a whole animal
  return(round(opt_res$par))
}

# 3. Run the Grid in Parallel
# We use future_map2_dbl to iterate over our grid of Targets and Spacings
plan(multisession)

cat("Starting optimization for 12 scenarios. This may take a few minutes...\n")

results_table <- sim_grid |> 
  mutate(
    Required_N = future_map2_dbl(
      target_dets, spacing_val, 
      ~find_optimal_N(.x, .y),
      .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE)
    )
  )

# 4. View the Final Results
print(results_table |> arrange(target_dets, n_transects))
```
